$schema_note: BuckPuck LED-driver calibration for the G6 arena. This file DOCUMENTS the transfer function used by js/arena-runner-g6.js (ledPercentToMv). Until issue #156 wires rig-config-driven loading, js/arena-runner-g6.js mirrors these values — keep the two in sync.
control: Controller 'Analog Out (0-5V)' BNC (J27, MCP4725 DAC), SET_AO_VOLTAGE 0xA0

units.output: millivolts (control voltage on the AO BNC)

polarity: inverted — lower mV = brighter; ~4.2 V = off, ~1.65 V = full (datasheet Fig. 3)

bench_findings_2026-07-08.note: With the raw datasheet curve (no floor), low inputs were below the LED's turn-on: 1-4% fully dark, 5% on-but-flickery, 6% solidly on.

remap.rule: input 1-100% -> raw 5-100% (linear); input 0% -> off. So input 1% == the raw 5% just-on level; input 100% == full.

accuracy: APPROXIMATE — datasheet-digitized + a bench turn-on floor, NOT photometric. Replace datasheet_curve_raw with measured (mv -> light) points for a real photometric calibration, and (per #156) point the rig config here.
